summary: DHH reflects on returning to in-person conferences after the COVID years and argues that physical gatherings produce far better discourse than online platforms like Twitter. He observes that people's ideological guards drop at in-person events, enabling genuine curiosity and good-faith debate that's nearly impossible online. He acknowledges the apparent irony given his decades of advocating remote work, but frames in-person events as battery-recharging complements to remote work rather than contradictions. He concludes that healing online community divisions requires periodic physical connection to build the good faith that sustains healthy digital interaction.
key_points: Twitter has become a reinforcement engine for existing beliefs and tribal identities, making good-faith debate nearly impossible
In-person events lower ideological guards and open minds to genuine curiosity and perspective change
Periodic in-person gatherings and remote work are complementary, not contradictory — events recharge the social batteries that sustain remote collaboration
Online community health depends on good faith that is built much faster through physical human connection
The post-COVID return to conferences has reinforced that some forms of intellectual exchange simply work better face-to-face
money_quotes: Twitter is a reinforcement engine for existing beliefs and tribes.
Everyone's guards are up so high when they engage in online, public forums.
The ideological guards are down, the mind is opened to new ideas, and most people show up with a genuine curiosity about the topics in play.

These industry events recharge social and intellectual batteries. Storing energy that can sustain working remotely and engaging with strangers over the internet in a healthier way for months afterwards.
The strength of online communities is drawn from the good faith of its participants.
Building that faith, broadly and deeply, just happens much faster when we tap into the human connections established by a disarming smile, eye contact, and the open minds that follow.
key_insight: Online discourse runs on a social battery that can only be recharged through in-person human connection, making periodic physical gatherings essential to sustaining healthy digital communities.
